title: Wedelia frustrata
intro:
There is no information available on the plant Wedelia frustrata. However, based on the similar-sounding name, it might be related to the plant Wedelia trilobata, also known as the Mexican Tarragon or Wedelia. 

Wedelia trilobata is a plant with medicinal properties, and it is used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of Wedelia trilobata include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can be beneficial in treating conditions such as digestive issues, fever, and skin problems.

The bioactive constituents of Wedelia trilobata include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Wedelia trilobata include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
uses: Wedelia frustrata is a plant species that has been studied for its potential medicinal properties. Research has shown that extracts from Wedelia frustrata may exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant may be used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Additionally, Wedelia frustrata has been found to have antimicrobial properties, making it a potential candidate for treating infections. Further studies are needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Wedelia frustrata for medicinal purposes.
benefits: Wedelia frustrata, also known as yellow daisy or four o'clock flower, is a plant species that has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal and health benefits. It has been used to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and skin conditions. constituents: Wedelia frustrata contains sesquiterpene lactones, primarily wedelolactone, which is responsible for its medicinal properties. parts: Wedelia frustrata, also known as yellow crownbeard, has been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Mexico. The plant's leaves are often used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin conditions. In traditional medicine, the leaves and flowers are used to make teas, infusions, or poultices for their medicinal properties. preparations: Wedelia frustrata, also known as spreading wedelia, is a type of flowering plant native to Central and South America. Herbal preparations made from the plant are traditionally used for various purposes, including reducing inflammation and relieving fever.
